definitions: A new architecture in React that allows developers to build applications that span both server and client, enabling server-side rendering and data fetching.
A feature in React that allows components to be rendered on the server, reducing the amount of JavaScript sent to the client and improving initial page load performance.
A paradigm in React development where certain components are executed exclusively on the server, often for data fetching or sensitive logic, and their output is streamed to the client.
examples: We are migrating our application to use React Server Components for better performance.
The new dashboard leverages React Server Components to fetch data directly on the server.
Understanding the lifecycle of React Server Components is crucial for optimizing their use.
